results.title: Borrowing Patterns of NLB Physical and E-books by Language and Age Categories in Past 10 Years
results.summary: From January 2016 to December 2025, the National Library Board recorded approximately 330 million physical and e-book loans. English-language titles accounted for 76% of total loans, followed by Chinese (21%), Malay (2%), and Tamil (1%). By age group, adult borrowers comprised 60% of loans, while children's books represented 36% and teenage books 4%. These figures reflect borrowing patterns across languages and demographics over the decade.

results.title: Commercial and Sectoral Opportunities for Singapore SMEs Following Timor-Leste's Accession to ASEAN
results.summary: Timor-Leste joined ASEAN on 26 October 2025 and is implementing reforms to create a transparent economic environment. While specific commercial opportunities are premature to identify, Enterprise Singapore will provide facilitation support for SMEs interested in setting up or expanding operations there. Eligible SMEs can access existing Government schemes, including the Market Readiness Assistance and Enterprise Financing Scheme, to assist with overseas market entry.

results.title: Committee of Supply Reporting Progress
results.summary: The Committee of Supply reported progress on the Estimates of Expenditure for the financial year 2026/2027. The Committee has requested leave to sit again tomorrow to continue its work. No specific measures, timelines, or eligibility rules were detailed in this progress report. The debate focused solely on the procedural status of the Estimates review and the scheduling of the next session.

results.title: Cooling-off Period for Re-polling in HDB Blocks That Failed HIP Voting
results.summary: The review of the Home Improvement Programme (HIP) voting process is ongoing to balance estate upgrades with resident decisions. Currently, a minimum of 75% support is required for HIP to proceed, as essential works like waste pipe replacement affect multiple residents. The Minister did not confirm a standard cooling-off period or timeline for re-polling in blocks failing by three votes or fewer, nor was a simple majority threshold adopted. Specific reasons for not adopting a simple majority were not explicitly stated beyond the impact on other residents.

results.title: Data on Singaporeans Above 65 Who Have Registered LPA
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development announced that Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA) Form 1 applications will remain free for all Singapore Citizens indefinitely, extending the fee waiver beyond 31 March 2026 to encourage early legacy planning. As of 20 February 2026, approximately 404,000 Singapore Citizens, or one in seven, have registered an LPA, with 197,000 individuals aged above 65 having done so. The Ministry will continue public education campaigns in collaboration with agencies like the Agency for Integrated Care and the People's Association, focusing on simplifying messaging and expanding language support. While the Office of the Public Guardian Online portal experienced performance issues during peak usage, the Ministry seeks specific feedback to address technical constraints and improve site capacity.

results.title: Declining Rates of Youth Blood Donors and Measures to Ensure Sustainability of Future National Blood Supply
results.summary: The Government is concerned about declining youth blood donor rates driven by a shrinking youth population and waning interest. To address this, the Health Sciences Authority (HSA) and Singapore Red Cross (SRC) are launching targeted campaigns with partners like PeanutsTM and ARTBOX, launching the YouthInspire volunteer club in June 2024, and holding a National Blood Dialogue in September 2025. Strategies include converting new donors to regulars, expanding workplace partnerships through "Adopt a Bloodbank" initiatives, and implementing evidence-based transfusion practices to optimize demand. These measures aim to recruit new donors, sustain participation, and ensure a stable national blood supply over the coming decade.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head O (Ministry of Health)
results.summary: Singapore has transitioned into a super-aged society with 20.7% of the population aged 65+ in June 2025, prompting the Ministry of Health to shift from episodic acute care to continuous multi-disciplinary models via Healthier SG and AgeWell SG. While addressing regional disparities in diabetes and hypertension, MOH clarified that MediSave remains a zero-sum scheme balancing chronic disease management against future hospitalisation episodes, resisting liberalisation to preserve the pot. The Minister reaffirmed that insurance riders primarily cover co-payments and deductibles rather than major inpatient costs, advising individuals to assess financial needs before purchasing. Concurrently, MOH is scaling AI infrastructure through NEHR and Next Generation EMR, strengthening local pipelines for allied health professionals, and integrating social prescriptions to combat senior isolation.

results.title: Extending Subsidised Rates for Home Fire Alarm Devices to All HDB Flats
results.summary: As of June 2025, approximately 17% of HDB flats have Home Fire Alarm Devices (HFADs) installed. Mandatory installation applies only to new premises since June 2018 and existing units undergoing fire safety alterations; pre-2018 flats are not mandated but encouraged. Existing owners accessing financial support include those under the Home Improvement Programme and the HDB's Enhancement for Active Seniors programme, with public rental tenants receiving free devices. The Minister noted that HFADs capable of sending mobile alerts are commercially available for procurement. The Government will continue reviewing effective fire safety solutions and associated financial support mechanisms for the public.

results.title: Enhanced Protection Measures for Students and Parents Against Disruptions in Outsourced Student Care Services
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the question regarding enhanced protection measures for outsourced student care services was previously addressed in the Ministry's response to Oral Parliamentary Questions Nos 3 to 6 on 2 March 2026. No new governance review, specific safeguards, or updated timelines were announced in this session. The transcript indicates that the matter has been handled via the existing parliamentary record rather than introducing fresh policy actions or operational constraints at this time.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head I (Ministry of Social and Family Development)
results.summary: The Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF) reaffirms its support for mandatory co-parenting programmes for divorcing couples and acknowledges the need to address funding reforms and employment resilience for families with persons with disabilities. A Taskforce chaired by Minister of State Goh Pei Ming has been appointed to tackle post-school cliff challenges, including long waiting times for placements and unaffordable Day Activity Centre fees. While MSF maintains its four core principles of family centricity, proactive upstream intervention, strength-based approaches, and whole-of-society partnership, it noted that specific questions regarding a single centralised support portal and expanded roles for learning support educators remain under consideration. The Ministry also highlighted the need for longitudinal research on social mobility drivers and guidance on Lasting Powers of Attorney registration fees, which are currently prohibitive for non-citizens.

results.title: Financial Viability Safeguards Required of Student Care Centre Operators
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that the question regarding financial viability safeguards for student care centre operators was previously addressed in the Ministry's response to Oral Parliamentary Questions Nos 3 to 6 on 2 March 2026. No new measures, monitoring indicators, or additional prudential steps such as fee collection safeguards or contingency planning were announced in this session. The transcript does not contain updated details on these specific operational requirements or the Ministry's current monitoring status beyond the referenced prior answer.

results.title: Framework for Determining When Economic Conditions Warrant Broad-based Payouts to Households
results.summary: The Government employs a framework for broad-based payouts that evaluates economic and inflation outlooks, fiscal injection sizes, specific household needs, and fiscal sustainability. This approach combines quantitative and qualitative assessments to ensure responsiveness to prevailing conditions while sharing economic growth benefits with Singaporeans. The Minister confirmed these factors guide payout decisions but did not specify a formal published framework or detailed key indicators beyond these general categories.

results.title: Lessons on Crowd Management, Shuttle Service Patterns and Communications from Recent Circle Line Closures
results.summary: For Circle Line tunnel strengthening works, the Land Transport Authority deployed crowd marshals, provided advance publicity, and launched replacement shuttle buses two weeks early to improve commuter familiarity and manage wait times. While scheduled closures for the North East Line renewal programme remain undecided, the Ministry will incorporate lessons from the Circle Line experience and commuter feedback to refine future alternative travel options. Suggestions regarding extending shuttle bus routes beyond impacted MRT sections and increasing train frequency on non-impacted lines have been noted and will be considered when planning supporting services for the North East Line works.

results.title: Investigations into Whether Singaporeans Served in Israeli Defence Forces
results.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) has no substantiated information confirming that any Singaporean served in the Israeli Defence Forces (IDF) in 2025, though it has requested verification from Israel without receiving a response. The Government maintains a zero-tolerance stance, stating that Singaporeans engaging in foreign armed conflicts will be dealt with sternly under existing laws, including the Internal Security Act for national security threats and potential deprivation of citizenship for dual nationals. While Section 125 of the Penal Code may apply depending on specific facts, the current legal framework is deemed adequate without immediate amendments. The MHA faces challenges in obtaining foreign cooperation to verify allegations, as other nations may not enforce disclosure of dual citizenship or military service details.

results.title: Impact of Physical Disconnection on Cross-Institutional Research Collaboration Between Autonomous Universities
results.summary: The Minister for Education stated that physical disconnection between Singapore's autonomous universities does not hinder cross-institutional research collaboration. Despite the absence of direct transit links between major campuses, autonomous universities maintain effective cooperation through joint research programmes, shared projects, and access to high-value equipment and facilities. The Ministry asserts that these existing arrangements successfully support Singapore's ambition to build a globally competitive research ecosystem without requiring new transit infrastructure.

results.title: Long-term Financial Sustainability of Autonomous Universities amid Evolving Global Competition
results.summary: Autonomous universities in Singapore ensure long-term financial sustainability through substantial government funding for operating and capital costs, supplemented by matching grants for endowment donations and investment returns. As autonomous entities, they possess the flexibility to borrow from capital markets to finance initiatives while actively managing costs via manpower optimisation, process enhancement, and digital transformation. These combined funding sources and cost-control measures provide the necessary financial stability and resilience to effectively respond to evolving global competition.

results.title: Measures to Monitor and Carry Out Enforcement on Littering in Public Parks
results.summary: The National Environment Agency and National Parks Board monitor public park cleanliness via ground surveillance and public feedback, targeting hotspots with persistent issues. Enforcement measures include increased patrol frequency, additional surveillance cameras at identified locations, and the deployment of educational banners to promote social responsibility. The Ministry does not currently plan to increase patrols broadly but focuses resources on areas with repeated littering complaints.

results.title: Official Data Collection for Traffic Accidents Involving Workers Transported in Course of Employment
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower excludes employees not performing work duties, such as those being transported to work, from workplace injury statistics to ensure data reflects risks directly related to work activities. However, employers must report all traffic accident injuries involving workers travelling between workplaces or using company-provided transport for commuting to MOM. This reporting requirement ensures that workers' livelihoods are protected and that a claim is automatically initiated upon employer submission, guaranteeing timely compensation regardless of whether a work injury compensation claim is filed.

results.title: Mental Preparation for National Service Enlistees and Usage Trends of SAF Whistle-blowing and Support Helplines in Past Five Years
results.summary: The Ministry of Defence prepares pre-enlistees for National Service through school-based information sessions, virtual visits to the Basic Military Training Centre, and LifeSG mobile application features. During Full-Time NS, servicemen access an established support system including counselling, safety, and feedback hotlines. Senior Minister of State for Defence, Mr Heng Chee How, noted that usage rates for these helplines have remained consistent over recent years. He directed further details on the comprehensive support system to his previous response to Parliamentary Questions from Mr Desmond Choo and Dr Wan Rizal on 7 March 2024. The Minister did not provide specific usage statistics or confirm whether current trends indicate increased trust or awareness.

results.title: Mechanisms for Early Detection of Wage or CPF Payment Defaults by Essential Service Vendors
results.summary: The Ministry of Manpower (MOM) and CPF Board utilize data-sharing mechanisms to detect wage or CPF payment defaults across all sectors, with enforcement actions taken against errant employers. For eligible local lower-income workers affected by employer insolvency, the Short-Term Relief Fund provides financial assistance, with disbursement taking approximately one to two months after claim verification. Additional support includes job matching and training via Workforce Singapore and SkillsFuture Singapore, alongside temporary financial aid under the SkillsFuture Jobseeker Support scheme for displaced workers. Lower-income households facing basic needs challenges may also access assistance through their respective Social Service Offices. The Government continues to review these measures to ensure prompt support for employees when issues arise.

results.title: Overhaul of NETS' QR Code Payment System to Create Fully Open, Interoperable Standard
results.summary: The Minister clarified that SGQR consolidates multiple payment schemes into a single label, allowing merchants and consumers to choose preferred providers without compromising competition. He stated that making all schemes fully interoperable would reduce market competition and negatively impact services and costs. Currently, NETS partners with other schemes to accept payments from 14 domestic providers and seven international schemes like DuitNow and QRIS, enabling seamless cross-border and domestic transactions. The Monetary Authority of Singapore will explore further opportunities to improve the payment experience, including supporting commercial interoperability arrangements, rather than mandating a fully open standard.

results.title: POFMA Action on Allegedly AI-generated YouTube Video on 17 January 2026 about Prime Minister Wong
results.summary: Regarding an allegedly AI-generated YouTube video from 17 January 2026 claiming Prime Minister Lawrence Wong's resignation is inevitable, the Minister for Digital Development and Information stated no POFMA correction direction was issued. This decision followed the removal of the content by YouTube for violating its community guidelines against misinformation and spam. While the Government will continue to work with platforms to investigate similar resurfacing accounts, it emphasized that POFMA is not the sole remedy against such disinformation campaigns. The Ministry highlighted that a vigilant public remains the primary defence, urging citizens to verify content through official sources rather than forwarding unverified material.

results.title: Penalties for Those Caught in Possession of, Producing or Disseminating Explicit Images of Actual Children versus AI-generated Material
results.summary: Under the Penal Code, possession, production, and dissemination of sexually explicit images of children carry mandatory imprisonment and discretionary caning. The 2025 Ministry of Home Affairs amendment clarifies that prosecution does not require proof an actual child was used, provided the image resembles a real child. This scope explicitly includes AI-generated material. The Government will not amend these offences to impose less severe penalties for AI-generated content compared to images of actual children, maintaining a strong stance on protecting children from sexual predation.

results.title: Pet Groomers Operating from Residential Addresses
results.summary: Over the past 12 months, the Urban Redevelopment Authority (URA) received one feedback case regarding smell and hygiene from a pet groomer operating in a private condominium. Following investigation, URA issued an advisory letter requiring the operator to implement mitigation measures, including installing air purifiers and conducting daily cleaning of the grooming area. The Government does not possess data on the total number of pet groomers operating from residential addresses. No further actions or outcomes were reported for this specific case.

results.title: Progress of Circle Line MRT Tunnel Reinforcement Works
results.summary: As of end-February 2026, approximately 50% of the required steel rings for Circle Line tunnel reinforcement works have been installed, with completion scheduled for 19 April 2026. The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed that the project remains on schedule and cannot be accelerated, as rushing complex works would compromise worker and commuter safety. While the Ministry continues to adjust train and shuttle bus services to minimize inconvenience, it declined requests to bring forward the timeline or provide fare rebates for affected commuters. The Minister stated that offering travel rebates during maintenance is not a common practice globally and that such disruptions are a necessary part of sustaining the efficiency, reliability, and financial sustainability of the ageing public transport network.

results.title: Police Reports Filed for Suspected Offences Under Part 9 of AMLA and Breakdown of Investigation Outcomes
results.summary: From 2016 to 2025, four police reports were filed for suspected offences under Part 9 of the Administration of Muslim Law Act. Outcomes included one case where a warning was issued following consultation with the Attorney-General's Chambers, one prosecution resulting in conviction, and two cases currently under ongoing investigation. No further action was taken in the remaining instances beyond these specific outcomes.

results.title: Progress on Achieving Social Service Sector Manpower Targets and Impact of S Pass and Employment Pass Changes on Hiring
results.summary: The social service sector is on track to meet its projected manpower target of adding 2,000 professionals between 2024 and 2029. This progress is supported by initiatives including new scholarships, training sponsorships, regular reviews of skills and salary guidelines, and awareness campaigns like the Year of Celebrating Social Service Professionals. The Minister stated that the increase in qualifying salaries for S Pass and Employment Pass holders is not expected to significantly impact manpower growth in this sector.

results.title: Proportion of Fires Arising from Electrical Sources and Cooking in Homes and Commercial Premises
results.summary: From 2023 to 2025, unattended cooking and electrical sources accounted for 68%, 65%, and 59% of residential fires, and 66%, 72%, and 67% of commercial fires respectively, with five and 11 residential and commercial incidents involving Fire Code breaches. The Singapore Civil Defence Force does not track compliance status for general batteries like power banks due to cost and verification difficulties, though AMD batteries are tracked and represent less than 5% of total fires despite higher injury risks. While the Ministry of Transport has moved a Bill regarding AMDs, the Ministry of Home Affairs and SCDF continue cross-agency outreach with the People's Association, schools, and Town Councils for enforcement and community education. The Minister noted that determining battery compliance post-fire is often difficult, and no specific plans for mandatory battery certification frameworks were detailed beyond existing regulatory approaches.

results.title: Purchase Incentives to Encourage Adoption of Electric Motorcycles
results.summary: The Acting Minister for Transport confirmed there are no plans to introduce upfront purchase incentives for electric motorcycles, citing that comparable pricing already exists for mass-market electric models versus internal combustion engine equivalents. The Minister attributed low adoption rates primarily to shorter driving ranges and insufficient infrastructure, such as a lack of adequate fast-charging or battery swap stations. Consequently, the Ministry will not address the perceived price gap through financial incentives at this time.

results.title: Providing Further Support to Local SMEs Struggling with Increasing Rent and Operating Costs
results.summary: The Government introduced a 40% Corporate Income Tax rebate for YA2026, capped at $30,000 with a minimum benefit of $1,500 for active companies employing at least one local person. Additional support includes CDC and SG60 Vouchers, which have facilitated over $2 billion in spending at heartland merchants over four years. Enterprises can access business grants like the Enterprise Development Grant and Productivity Solutions Grant for transformation, while internationalisation scheme support levels increase to 70% for SMEs and 50% for non-SMEs. Higher loan limits are available under the Enterprise Financing Scheme for trade and fixed assets. The Ministry of Trade and Industry also facilitates restructuring, scaling down, or exiting operations through collaboration with trade associations and chambers to support economic renewal and worker transitions.

results.title: Seniors, Children and Pedestrians Injured in Accidents Involving Active Mobility Devices
results.summary: From 2023 to 3Q 2025, approximately 590 on-road accidents involving personal mobility devices (PMDs), power-assisted bicycles (PABs), and personal mobility aids (PMAs) resulted in injuries. Among these, 19 incidents involved pedestrians, 181 involved seniors aged 65 and above, and 24 involved children aged 12 and below; these categories may overlap. Additionally, 10 fatal accidents occurred on the road during this period, while no fatalities were recorded for off-road incidents. Data at this granularity is not collected for the approximately 120 off-road accidents, which are generally less severe.

results.title: Simulated Total Defence Exercise for Complete Communications Blackout Affecting Internet, Cellular and Broadband Networks
results.summary: Exercise SG Ready simulated a partial disruption to power and digital connectivity rather than a total blackout, concluding that layered redundancy across different channels minimizes the risk of simultaneous failure. Contingency messaging relies on the Singapore Civil Defence Force public warning system, free-to-air television, local radio, and the gov.sg WhatsApp, with FM radio serving as a backup if digital and TV channels fail. Updates are also available at community nodes for those without access to standard broadcasting equipment. The Ministry confirmed there are no current plans to distribute battery-powered FM radios to every household, though it will continue to review emergency preparedness systems and evolve exercise scenarios based on emerging challenges.

results.title: Spousal Employment as Barrier to Retaining Foreign Academic Talent at Autonomous Universities
results.summary: The Minister for Education confirmed that spouses of foreign academics and researchers at autonomous universities can access various work passes, including Employment Pass, S Pass, Work Permit, and Letter of Consent for Dependant's Pass holders who are business owners or spouses of ONE Pass holders. Autonomous universities provide support such as sharing internal job openings, guiding job search platforms, and advising on work pass requirements. The Minister did not disclose specific assessments on the extent of spousal employment difficulties contributing to attrition or whether this is considered a barrier to achieving a globally competitive research ecosystem. No specific policy measures to address this factor were detailed in the response.

results.title: Statistics on Bullying Cases in National Service in Past Three Years and Protocols to Ensure Victim Safety During Investigations
results.summary: The Ministry of Defence and Singapore Armed Forces (SAF) maintain a zero-tolerance stance on workplace harassment, investigating all reports and disciplining perpetrators accordingly. Pre-enlistment preparation involves school sessions, virtual visits, and buddy systems during Basic Military Training to help recruits recognise stress signs and cope with challenges. Throughout full-time National Service, commanders conduct regular interviews to identify potential problems, while para-counsellors in every unit and psychologists in divisions provide frontline support. Accessible hotlines for counselling, safety, and feedback are available for those uncomfortable sharing openly. Where harassment occurs, the SAF arranges psychological care, redeployment, or distancing measures to minimise distress for affected personnel and prevent further harassment during investigations.

results.title: Statistics on Public Park Littering Enforcement and Recalcitrant Offenders and Public Identification Practices as Deterrent Measure
results.summary: In 2025, the National Environment Agency (NEA) and National Parks Board (NParks) took enforcement action against 48 littering offenders in public parks, identifying two as repeat offenders. While surveillance cameras monitor cleanliness hotspots, NEA has not yet publicly displayed closed-circuit television footage of offenders to aid identification, though it retains the option to do so for egregious cases requiring community support. The Minister confirmed that no images have been displayed to date.

results.title: Strengthening of Procedures to Prevent Recurrence of Recent Episode of Erroneous Issuance of Speeding Tickets
results.summary: The Minister for Home Affairs confirmed that no drivers had their licences suspended due to the 1,523 erroneous speeding tickets issued between October and December 2025. To prevent recurrence, the Traffic Police have strengthened procedures by conducting monthly audits on all speed enforcement cameras and implementing additional verification steps following maintenance work. These operational adjustments aim to reduce the risk of technical errors in future enforcement actions.

results.title: Status of Joint Multi-Mission Ship Programme That Was to Replace Endurance-class Landing Platform Docks
results.summary: The Coordinating Minister for Public Services and Minister for Defence confirmed that the Republic of Singapore Navy's Endurance-class Landing Ships Tank remain operationally safe and fit-for-mission through comprehensive maintenance and life-extension programmes. The Minister stated these vessels will continue to serve Singapore well into the 2030s. Regarding the Joint Multi-Mission Ship (JMMS) programme announced in 2014 to replace the Endurance-class, the Ministry indicated it will provide further updates on the replacement timeline closer to when the vessels are due to be replaced.

results.title: Support Framework for Schools and Teachers on Addressing Student Exposure to Manosphere-related Online Content
results.summary: The Ministry of Education (MOE) employs a holistic, values-based approach to address student exposure to manosphere-related content by integrating gender equality and critical thinking into the curriculum. Through subjects like Character and Citizenship Education, students develop digital literacy to identify manipulation, bias, and echo chambers while learning to counter gender-based violence. Teachers receive specific training and resources to guide students in evaluating online media messages and fostering respectful relationships based on mutual respect and empathy across all school levels.

results.title: Support System Adequacy Indicators for Assessments and Interventions of At-risk Students
results.summary: The Ministry of Education does not rely on key performance indicators but enforces baseline requirements for all educational institutions to maintain essential suicide support capabilities. Schools and Institutes of Higher Learning (IHLs) employ trained educators and counsellors who conduct suicide risk assessments, provide initial guidance, and adhere to professional ethical standards. High-risk students are referred to multi-disciplinary community mental health teams, specifically the Response, Early Intervention and Assessment in Community Mental Health (REACH) and Community Health Assessment Team (CHAT), or hospital emergency services. Peer support structures encourage students to seek help from trusted adults, while parental consent is sought when necessary for further intervention. The Ministry continues to strengthen these systems through collaboration with community partners to ensure adequate support in schools and other settings.

results.title: Take-up Rate by Logistics Providers of Courier Hub Scheme
results.summary: The Minister stated that the take-up rate for the Courier Hub Scheme was previously addressed in a reply to Ms Valerie Lee on 25 February 2026. Regarding enforcement, the Ministry clarified that Town Councils may enforce by-laws under the Town Councils Act against activities obstructing common property, such as parcel sorting in void decks. No new penalties or specific enforcement protocols were introduced during this session. The transcript does not provide current take-up statistics or details on future enforcement measures beyond existing statutory powers.

results.title: Top 10 Peak Hour Traffic Congestion Junctions in Singapore and Measures towards Congestion Relief
results.summary: As of January 2026, major junctions near expressways and arterial roads leading to commercial zones typically exhibit the highest peak hour traffic volumes. The Land Transport Authority manages flow using the adaptive Green Link Determining System, which adjusts signals based on real-time conditions. The agency will continue leveraging predictive artificial intelligence to enhance traffic management. Specific data on the top 10 congested junctions and plans to review traffic light timings at those specific locations were not provided in the response.

results.title: Timelines, Constraints and Safeguards for Resumption of Crow-shooting Operations
results.summary: The National Parks Board (NParks) will progressively resume crow shooting operations from the second half of March 2026, following an announcement on 23 February 2026. Licensed wildlife management contractors will execute these operations under strict protocols developed jointly with the Ministry of Home Affairs and Singapore Police Force, ensuring shotgun pellets are directed upwards and zones are cordoned off with signs and additional personnel. Site prioritization considers public feedback volume and technical feasibility, allowing NParks to refine safety measures before scaling up. This shooting component complements existing management efforts like trapping, removal, and nest removal within a holistic strategy. Residents are urged not to feed crows or pigeons and to keep the environment clean to support liveable estates.

results.title: Committee of Supply – Head X (Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth)
results.summary: The Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth (MCCY) will foster a "we first" national identity through three pillars: expanding common spaces, encouraging care and contribution, and rallying the nation for inclusivity. To address youth isolation and social fragmentation, MCCY will co-design new open, accessible community spaces in heartlands with the Ministry of National Development, treating the need to gather as essential social infrastructure. The ministry will deepen multiculturalism by launching a new $20 million Multicultural Arts Programme Grant and increasing Artist-in-School Scheme support from 70% to 100% of costs, capped at $25,000 annually per school. Regarding heritage and religious infrastructure, MUIS will collaborate with mosque leaders to preserve historic sites, while the acquisition of 38 Oxley Road proceeds with a holistic study to optimize public education value. Additionally, MCCY commits up to $30 million to support the co-operative movement over the next decade and will launch a new committee to guide the Indian community's development.

results.title: Updated Assessment on Impact on Singapore's Economy and Businesses from New 15% Tariffs
results.summary: The Ministry assesses the immediate economic impact of the new 15% US tariff on Singapore as limited, noting that the current 10% section 122 tariff remains largely unchanged. While the 15% rate was announced for 150 days starting 24 February 2026, an official directive for the increase has not yet been issued, and further investigations under sections 301 or 232 remain uncertain. To support affected sectors like precision engineering, the Government raised Business Adaptation Grant (BizAdapt) support to 70% and utilizes the Singapore Economic Resilience Taskforce (SERT) to gather industry feedback. Singapore continues to diversify trade links through 29 Free Trade Agreements and emerging markets to safeguard economic interests amid global trade tensions.

results.title: Updated Mental Health Studies Across Demographic Groups
results.summary: The Minister for Health confirmed that plans to conduct a new national mental health study across all demographic groups are already underway, superseding the last published study from 2016. Further details regarding the study's scope and timeline will be announced soon. The Coordinating Minister for Social Policies did not provide specific eligibility criteria or operational constraints for the upcoming research initiative.

results.title: Update on Heavy Vehicle Zero Emissions Scheme, Including Public Trials of Hydrogen-powered Heavy Vehicles and Developing Refuelling Infrastructure
results.summary: As of end-February 2026, 119 electric heavy vehicles were registered under the Heavy Vehicle Zero Emissions Scheme launched on 1 January 2026. The Acting Minister for Transport stated that electrification remains the most commercially and technologically viable decarbonisation pathway for the land transport sector. While the government will continue to monitor technological developments, no specific timeline for public trials of hydrogen-powered heavy vehicles or details on refuelling infrastructure development were provided. The feasibility of adopting hydrogen and other cleaner energy technologies is currently under study.

results.title: Updated School Protocols for Suicide Postvention Response and Trauma Management
results.summary: Following a suspected student suicide, schools deploy CARE teams comprising trained teachers, counsellors, and MOE personnel to provide immediate psychological support. Affected students receive emotional guidance and are directed to trusted adults, while parents are sensitively informed of the passing without specific details to protect bereaved family privacy. Schools actively monitor for contagion risks and intervene as necessary, ensuring staff access to professional MOE counselling services. The Minister emphasized that speculation on the cause of death is discouraged, and resources are provided to help families monitor their children's well-being.

results.title: Uptake of Hawkers’ Productivity Grant for Adoption of Automation and Digital Solutions
results.summary: As of 31 December 2025, the Hawkers' Productivity Grant (HPG) has supported approximately 1,150 stallholders in adopting automation and digital solutions, with two-thirds of stalls now accepting e-payments. Complementing HPG, the Productive Hawker Centres (PHC) programme offers up to 70% funding for centralised dishwashing in existing centres and up to 50% in new ones, a measure already implemented in all 16 post-2011 new centres and 10 existing centres. The National Environment Agency (NEA) will continue reviewing its programmes to further support technological adoption and overhead cost reduction.
